bin.description: Matches the `bin` field set in package.json

dependencies.description: Matches the `dependencies` field set in package.json

dev.description: A flag specifying this package is strictly part of the `devDependencies` tree.

devOptional.description: A flag specifying this package is a `dev` dependency *and* an `optional` dependency of a non-dev dependency.

engines.description: Matches the `engines` field set in package.json

hasInstallScript.description: A flag to indicate that this package has a `preinstall`, `install`, or `postinstall` script.

hasShrinkwrap.description: A flag to indicate that this package has an `npm-shrinkwrap.json` file.

inBundle.description: A flag to indicate that this package is a bundled dependency.

integrity.description: A `sha512` or `sha1` SRI for the artifact that was unpacked in this location. For git dependencies, this is the commit hash.

license.description: Matches the `license` field set in package.json

optional.description: A flag to indicate this this package is either an `optional` dependency of a `dev` dependency, or strictly part of the `optionalDependencies` tree and not a `dev` dependency.

optionalDependencies.description: Matches the `optionalDependencies` field set in package.json

resolved.description: For registry sources, this is path of the tarball relative to the registry URL. If the tarball URL isn't on the same server as the registry URL then this is a complete URL. For bundled dependencies, this is not included, regardless of source.

version.description: This is a specifier that uniquely identifies this package and should be usable in fetching a new copy of it.

link.description: A flag to indicate that this is a symbolic link. If this is present, no other fields are specified, since the link target will also be included in the lockfile.

packages.description: A mapping of package locations to an object containing information about that package. The root project is typically listed with a key of `""`, and all other packages are listed with their relative paths from the root project folder.
